candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: This D&A row is explicitly tied to SG&A, not Cost of revenue. It does not belong to the selected COGS bucket.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization Expense in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: This D&A row is explicitly tied to R&D, not Cost of revenue. It does not belong to the selected COGS bucket.
This D&A row is explicitly tied to Administration, not Cost of revenue. It does not belong to the selected COGS bucket.

variables.notes: No operating D&A row related to Cost of revenue / COGS is visible above the financial section. The visible D&A rows all belong to SG&A/R&D/Admin, so nothing should be added separately to COGS.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Although relevant to SG&A, it appears as a sub-component under the selected operating overhead rows rather than a separate same-level expense bucket. Adding it separately would risk double counting against the selected parent rows.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation & Amortization Expense in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: This appears to be a component of the selected 'Research and development' row. Also, 'Research and development' is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, so adding R&D D&A separately would distort the intended SG&A-minus-R&D construction.
Although relevant to Administration, it appears to be a component nested under the selected 'Administration' parent row, so it is already embedded and should not be added separately.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket is composed of the parent rows 'Sales and Marketing', 'Research and development', and 'Administration'. The visible D&A rows are clearly bucket-specific sub-components beneath those selected parent rows, not separate same-level expenses to add later. In particular, the R&D D&A row should not be added because the parent R&D row is already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD.
